cover.title: THE MIST
cover.subtitle: The one who believes in people to the end, even when the evidence has already arrived.
cover.tagline: You understand too much, and sometimes protect yourself too late.

profile_scores.caption: High warmth and tolerance, low drive and expression. Understanding to the end, believing to the end.

short_summary: You don't give up on people easily. Once you trust someone, you keep believing in the good side you once saw.
Your pain often comes from knowing something is wrong, but waiting too long anyway.
What changes love for you is learning to include yourself in the relationship, not only the other person's story.

full_report.headline: THE MIST
full_report.intro: You don't give up on people easily. Once you like someone, you keep watching them for a long time, and once you trust someone, you trust them for a long time. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why you understand people so deeply, why you leave too late, and how love changes when you finally take your own side too.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You don't give up on people easily.
Once you like someone, you keep watching them for a long time. Once you trust someone, you trust them for a long time.
So people may see you as gentle or easygoing. But that is not the whole picture.
Your will may be stronger than people realize.
Once you make up your mind, it lasts. You don't turn your back easily either.
The difficulty is that this strength can keep you holding on after the relationship has stopped caring for you.
You may hold onto someone long after part of you knows it is hurting.
You may keep trying to understand a relationship that is no longer moving.
You may keep searching for another meaning after the answer has already started to show.
Because when you look at someone, you don't just see one action.
You see their circumstances too. Their emotions too. Their wounds too.
So leaving can take time. Understanding their story can make your own hurt easier to postpone.
chapters.profile_note: High Warmth · High Tolerance, Low Drive · Low Expression. Understanding to the end, believing to the end.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat: THE BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT CURVE
body: At first, there may be a small discomfort. Something feels off. Something hurts a little.
But you let it pass because one difficult moment can happen.
It happens again. You understand the reason again. You make room for it again.
Then the pattern begins to feel harder to ignore.
You may still keep believing because people make mistakes, circumstances can be difficult, and there were good moments too.
Over time, you may realize you were carrying more hope for the relationship than the other person was showing through action.
chapters.highlight: The pain is not that you saw nothing. It is that what you saw kept getting pushed behind one more reason to wait.
chapters.pattern_name: THE BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT CURVE

chart.title: THE BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT CURVE
chart.description: The pattern of keeping giving second chances even after doubt has set in

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: You may not trust everyone easily. But once you see something good in someone, you can keep trusting that version of them for a long time.
items.truth_short: You may keep trusting the good side you once saw, even when the present looks different.

items.belief: Let's just wait a little longer.
items.truth: Sometimes waiting gives a person room to grow. Sometimes you have already waited long enough, and another delay only keeps your own decision out of reach.
items.truth_short: Waiting can be generous, but it can also delay a decision you already understand.

items.belief: I'm not someone with much desire.
items.truth: You may want love, care, and a clear place in someone's life more than you admit. The issue is not wanting too much. It is staying quiet about those needs for too long.
items.truth_short: You may want to feel loved and valued, while asking for very little.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You can be drawn to people with a complicated story.
A hard past. A difficult situation. Feelings they have not worked through yet.
Their story can help you understand why they act the way they do.
But understanding the reason does not mean you have to keep accepting the hurt.
chapters.highlight: Someone can give you a reason for their behavior. A caring relationship also makes room for your experience.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need is not an even greater ability to understand. You already bring plenty of that.
What may help is one honest question.
"I understand that they are having a hard time. But what is this costing me?"
That question can bring your own experience back into the relationship.
It does not mean you care less. It means your care finally includes you too.
chapters.action_sentence: Let your understanding include what the relationship is costing you.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You know how to forgive.
You know how to wait, make room, and try to trust again.
What you may learn next is how to notice where understanding needs a boundary.
Knowing why someone acts that way does not require you to keep being hurt by it.
When that line becomes clearer, the relationship has more room to become healthier.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time understanding other people's stories. The next step is learning how to protect your own place in the relationship sooner.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.description: They may tell you about a painful past early on. Their story matters. But being hurt before and treating you well now are still separate questions.

items.type_title: Someone Whose Life Is Still Unsettled
items.description: Their life is always about to become stable soon. You may keep waiting for a version of them that is not ready yet. Standing beside a problem is not the same as watching them take responsibility for it.

items.type_title: Someone Who Is Warm Only Sometimes
items.description: They disappear, then return with enough warmth to restore your hope. Occasional tenderness can feel powerful, but everyday care is what makes a relationship dependable.

items.type_title: Someone Who Relies on Your Understanding
items.description: At first they appreciate how deeply you understand them. Later, that appreciation can turn into an expectation that you will keep making room without receiving the same care.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Some of these signs may have appeared early. You may have answered them with understanding before checking your own hurt.

signal_strength.label: You understand on their behalf, instead of reacting to uncomfortable behavior
You think of their circumstances before your own hurt feelings
The same problem keeps returning, and you keep making room for it
You see their potential more than their actual behavior

chapters.core_signal: As the evidence grows, hope may keep growing beside it, making the pattern harder to act on.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: They're considerate without needing it explained.
checklist.description: Care appears in their behavior, not only after you explain why you are hurt.

checklist.title: Apologies turn into action.
checklist.description: Their apology is followed by a real effort to do something differently.

checklist.title: Your feelings matter too.
checklist.description: Your feelings receive as much attention as the reasons behind theirs.

checklist.title: You feel reassured.
checklist.description: You do not have to stay in uncertainty just to prove that you understand.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone whose circumstances always seem to explain why they cannot show up yet. You may keep waiting because their story makes sense. But a relationship cannot depend only on how well you understand why someone is not ready.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone whose care is easy to see. They repair what they can, keep promises more often than not, and do not make you carry the whole meaning of the relationship alone.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone with pain, complexity, or unfinished parts. That does not decide the ending. What matters is whether their story is followed by responsibility, consideration, and change.
sections.next_signs: This time, the familiar urge to wait may return sooner. You may notice the hurt, the repetition, and the voice that says, "Maybe I should give it more time." That voice is worth hearing, but it does not have to make the decision alone.
sections.try_this: When you feel yourself explaining the same hurt again, write down what happened without adding the reasons for it. Then ask what you would want for someone you care about in the same situation. Use that answer to bring your own needs back into view.
sections.final_line: What matters is not one answer. It is whether understanding is matched by care, responsibility, and change.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to understand people. What's left is learning how to protect yourself before understanding turns into leaving yourself out.
action_items: This week, ask what your understanding is costing you in one relationship.
Write down what happened before adding the other person's reasons.
Notice whether apologies are followed by different behavior.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to understand people. The next report covers why you keep finding meaning even in relationships that have ended, and how you can learn to protect yourself faster.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
